topic: Innovative Speech-Based Deep Learning Approaches for Parkinson's Disease Classification: A Systematic Review
topic_definition: Speech-based deep learning for Parkinson's disease classification is the use of neural models to analyze speech signals and distinguish speech associated with Parkinson's disease from non-Parkinsonian speech for screening or diagnostic support.

summary_topics.description: Innovative Speech-Based Deep Learning Approaches for Parkinson's Disease Classification: A Systematic Review

required.criterion: Uses deep learning (DL) methods for Parkinson’s disease classification using speech data.
Uses speech (vocal features) as the primary data modality.
May examine both vocal features and linguistic features (allowed).

exclusion_criteria.criterion: Duplicate study record (duplicates removed across databases).
Uses traditional methods such as GMMs (Gaussian Mixture Models).
Uses traditional methods such as HMMs (Hidden Markov Models).
Uses machine learning without deep learning (ML without DL).
Focuses on neurodegenerative diseases in general rather than specifically on Parkinson’s disease.
Literature review paper (other literature reviews discarded).
Uses types of data other than speech.
Relies on non-speech-related features such as gait.
Relies on non-speech-related features such as handwriting.
